instruction: Use change-based window motion, not raw window magnitude, as the input to the global shell.

problem.name: magnitude dominance
problem.symptom: W3 frequency remains dominant even after equalization.
problem.cause: The model is still using raw W1/W2/W3 values instead of their motion/change.
problem.correction: DOF should be inferred from how domains change over time, not from which domain has the largest units.

code_changes.name: equalize_domains
code_changes.code:
def equalize_domains(w_delta, eps=1e-12):
    """
    Equalize W1/W2/W3 by their motion/change, not raw magnitude.
    Input should already be delta values: [dW1, dW2, dW3].
    Output is dimensionless participation in [0, 1] with sum approximately 1.
    """
    W = np.abs(np.array(w_delta, dtype=np.float64))
    total = float(np.sum(W) + eps)
    return W / total

code_changes.name: normalize_to_global_shell
code_changes.code:
def normalize_to_global_shell(w_delta, eps=1e-12):
    W_eq = equalize_domains(w_delta, eps=eps)
    total = float(np.sum(W_eq) + eps)
    shell = W_eq / total
    shell_magnitude = float(np.linalg.norm(W_eq))
    return shell, shell_magnitude, W_eq

details: Compute current = np.array([W1_amp, W2_phase, W3_freq], dtype=np.float64).
If prev is None, set w_delta = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0]).
Use w_delta as the input to compute_channel_ratios(w_delta).
Do not use raw current values as input to channel ratio computation.
code_changes.code_hint:
current = np.array([W1_amp, W2_phase, W3_freq], dtype=np.float64)

if prev is None:
    w_delta = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0], dtype=np.float64)
else:
    w_delta = current - prev

ratio_data = compute_channel_ratios(w_delta)
prev = current

important_note.first_frame: The first frame has no previous state, so deltas are zero. Ignore it in plots or allow it to appear as initialization.
important_note.phase: If W2 remains near zero after this patch, the phase metric itself needs improvement, not the shell model.

plot_updates.title: Window Delta Motion W1/W2/W3
plot_updates.purpose: show which dynamic domain is actually moving

plot_updates.title: Domain Equalized W1/W2/W3 Values
plot_updates.expected: values should no longer be permanently dominated by W3 unless W3 truly has most of the motion

plot_updates.title: Global Shell W1/W2/W3 Participation
plot_updates.expected: participation should shift over time based on window motion

plot_updates.title: ch7 White Coupling Intensity
plot_updates.expected: nonzero when all three delta domains participate in the same frame

interpretation_rules.dominant_W1_delta: amplitude motion controls the current relation
interpretation_rules.dominant_W2_delta: phase motion controls the current relation
interpretation_rules.dominant_W3_delta: frequency motion controls the current relation
interpretation_rules.balanced_shell: all three windows are participating in the whole
interpretation_rules.high_ch7_intensity: strong white coupling / balanced three-domain motion
interpretation_rules.low_ch7_intensity: one or more domains weak relative to whole

success_conditions: global shell is based on deltas, not raw values
W3 no longer dominates only because it is measured in Hz
ch7 intensity represents balanced three-domain motion
plots show dynamic participation instead of fixed unit dominance

next_patch_after_success.name: axis_projection_8way_v1
next_patch_after_success.purpose: Add diagonal axis direction measurement once delta-based shell behavior is stable.
